reason: Round02 fixes the main internal paragraph-gap symptom by grouping aligned body paragraphs into a body flow, and it renders the main sidebar navigation as rotated text. Product-quality acceptance is still not claimed because the translated body finishes much earlier than the source and overall source-vs-output visual similarity remains below the final aesthetic bar.

dimensions.evidence: Round02 uses layout_policy.flow_grouping.body and font_profiles.body_flow. The output no longer leaves a large blank tail inside each source paragraph box, but the final y-span is still shorter than source because Chinese text is more compact.
Body paragraphs are placed in one flowing region with paragraph separators, so blank space is concentrated after the article rather than between active paragraphs.
The large blank area after the last translated paragraph is classified separately from internal paragraph gaps and is allowed as a non-blocking residual issue.
The active red sidebar title is rendered as rotated text instead of stacked one-character lines. Short inactive labels remain visually narrow because the source slots are narrow.
Overall source-vs-output visual acceptance remains blocked because text y-coverage and typographic rhythm are still visibly different from the English source.
